title: Design a secret rotation strategy for a microservices architecture
description: Given a microservices setup with various secret types, the skill should produce a rotation strategy with schedules, methods, and zero-downtime considerations.
prompt:
Design a secret rotation strategy for our microservices platform:

- 12 microservices running in Kubernetes
- PostgreSQL database with shared credentials across 4 services
- Redis with a single AUTH password
- 3 third-party API keys (Stripe, SendGrid, Twilio)
- TLS certificates managed manually, expiring in 2 months
- JWT signing key shared across auth service and 3 consumer services
- All secrets currently stored as Kubernetes Secrets (base64 encoded)
- No vault infrastructure in place

assertions.description: Must recommend implementing a vault or managed secret service
Must define rotation schedules for different secret types
Must address zero-downtime rotation strategy (dual credentials or rolling)
Must recommend dynamic/short-lived secrets for database credentials
Must address TLS certificate automation before the 2-month expiry

title: Design a HashiCorp Vault integration for Kubernetes workloads
description: Given a Kubernetes deployment, the skill should provide a vault integration pattern including agent injection, policy design, and secret engine configuration.
prompt:
We want to integrate HashiCorp Vault with our Kubernetes cluster for secret management. We have:

- 3 namespaces: production, staging, development
- Each namespace has 4-6 services that need database credentials
- Services are written in Go, Python, and Node.js
- We need to support both sidecar injection and direct API access
- PostgreSQL and MongoDB databases need dynamic credentials
- Some services need PKI certificates for mTLS

Design the Vault integration architecture.

assertions.description: Must configure Kubernetes authentication method for Vault
Must describe Vault Agent sidecar injection pattern
Must define Vault policies scoped per namespace
Must configure database secret engines for dynamic credentials
Must address PKI secret engine for mTLS certificate generation
